selfHosted.title: Self-Hosted Guide
selfHosted.subtitle: Everything you need to host Seogard on your own server.

gettingStarted.heading: Getting Started
gettingStarted.prereq: Prerequisites: Docker and Docker Compose installed on your server.
gettingStarted.stepEnv: Copy and configure the environment file
gettingStarted.mongoPasswordDesc: MongoDB password — pick a strong one
gettingStarted.stepOpen: Open http://localhost:3000 and create your admin account
gettingStarted.firstAccount: The first account created becomes the administrator (owner). Registration then closes automatically — subsequent members are added by invitation.
gettingStarted.singleEnv: A single .env file for everything (web + crawler). MongoDB and Redis connections are handled automatically by Docker.

architecture.heading: Architecture
architecture.text: The Web serves the dashboard and the API. The Workers crawl pages with Playwright (headless browser). Redis distributes pages to the workers. MongoDB stores the data.

sizing.heading: Sizing
sizing.intro: Each crawler worker uses ~1 GB of RAM. MongoDB needs a RAM cache to perform well. The rest (~2 GB) is shared between Redis, the web server and the OS.
sizing.formula: Formula: MongoDB cache = server RAM - (workers × 1 GB) - 2 GB
sizing.cacheDefault: The MongoDB cache defaults to 2 GB in the docker-compose. Adjust it to your server by editing docker-compose.self-hosted.yml → --wiredTigerCacheSizeGB X.

workers.heading: Adjusting workers
workers.text: Change the number of workers to match your server resources:

email.heading: Email notifications
email.optional: Optional — without email configuration, Seogard still works but sends no alerts and no password resets.
email.step2: Verify your domain in the Resend dashboard

oauth.heading: OAuth login
oauth.optional: Optional — without OAuth, users log in with email + password.
oauth.intro: Add the keys to your .env to enable each provider:

whitelisting.heading: Crawler whitelisting
whitelisting.text: If your sites are behind a WAF (Cloudflare, Akamai, etc.), you must whitelist your server's IP.

update.heading: Updating
update.text: MongoDB data persists in a Docker volume. No data loss during an update.

license.heading: License
license.text1: Seogard is distributed under the Business Source License 1.1 (BSL). You can use it for free for internal use (monitoring your own sites). Using it as a competing commercial service is prohibited.
license.text2: Each release automatically converts to Apache 2.0 after 3 years.

rules.title: SEO rules
rules.subtitle: {count} rules checked on every crawl.
rules.monitoringDesc: Compare the current state with the previous crawl. Detect what broke, changed or disappeared. Trigger alerts.
rules.auditDesc: Check the absolute state of every page on every crawl. No comparison with the past — they flag best practices that are not followed.

fiche.whyHeading: Why it hurts SEO and AI
fiche.notProblemHeading: When it is NOT a problem
fiche.scanQuestion: Is this issue on your site?
fiche.scanDiff: Seogard compares raw HTML and the JavaScript render
fiche.scanFallback: Enter a URL: Seogard compares the raw HTML and the JavaScript-rendered page and tells you within 30 seconds whether the issue is present. Free, no credit card.
fiche.signalText: Seogard continuously compares the raw HTML (what Google indexes) and the JavaScript-rendered page (what you see) on every page, and alerts you the moment this issue appears.
fiche.stickyText: What about your site? Check this issue in 30 seconds — free, no credit card.

author.role: Vue.js Lead Dev · founder of Seogard
